title: "make vendor" removes dependencies
body:
I'm trying to update vendoring with `make vendor` as required to [add new dependencies](https://github.com/containers/skopeo/blob/master/README.md#dependencies-management) and what I'm observing seems pretty strange.

First all repositories referenced in `vendor.conf` are being cloned. But in the end those where `vndr` is reporting `WARNING: package xyz is unused, consider removing it from vendor.conf` are being removed.

Am I doing something fully wrong?

This is the result of the `make vendor` call:
```
vndr -whitelist '^github.com/containers/image/docs/.*'
2019/01/08 00:21:25 Collecting initial packages
```

comments.body: I can't reproduce this on the latest master branch. Did you change any files? If the state is clean and the issue persists, it might be worth installing the latest `vndr`.

On my Arch system getting the latest `vndr` hasn't changed anything. I've only changed `vendor.conf` like this
```
-github.com/containers/image f0cbc16b444d729362c78244c715b45bf4019b71
+github.com/containers/image master https://github.com/silvanoc/image
```
and
```
+github.com/coreos/pkg v4
```
at the end of the file.

Just in order to reduce the differences with your probable setup I've tested on a Fedora container (a rootless one started with `podman`! :wink:) with the same result.

I'm getting the same error even when going back to the HEAD of current master.

Am I missing any preparation steps?
I cannot think of anything else than a dirty git branch or a buggy `vndr`. Did you try it with the latest `vndr`?

This is what I did in the containerized test:

```
dnf install git golang make
go get github.com/LK4D4/vndr
git clone https://github.com/containers/skopeo
cd skopeo
export PATH=$PATH:$HOME/go/bin
make vendor
```

So no dirty git branch or old `vndr`.

Are you trying to reproduce it on a container or any other "clean" environment too?

@vrothberg Let me document a setup that should help you reproducing the issue with Docker/Podman. It should ensure that we are both running on the same clean environment.

Create a Dockerfile with following content:
```
FROM fedora

RUN dnf install -y make git golang
RUN go get github.com/LK4D4/vndr
RUN git clone https://github.com/containers/skopeo
WORKDIR $HOME/skopeo
```

Build the corresponding image: 
```
docker build -t skopeo-vendor .
```

And run following container on the resulting image:
```
docker run --rm skopeo-vendor bash -c ' \
    PATH=$PATH:$HOME/go/bin make vendor ; \
    echo ; \
    grep "github.com/urfave/cli" cmd/skopeo/main.go &>/dev/null && echo "cmd/skopeo/main.go imports github.com/urfave/cli" ; \
    grep "github.com/urfave/cli" vendor.conf &>/dev/null && echo "vendor.conf references github.com/urfave/cli" ; \
    ls vendor/github.com/urfave &>/dev/null || echo -e "\n!!! github.com/urfave/cli is missing in vendor !!!"'
```

@Silvanoc indeed it helped :) It's import that your source directory is located in `$GOPATH/src/github.com/containers/skopeo` as described in the README. But I must admit that it's surprising that `vndr` ends up with those warnings.

If that works, feel free to close the issue.

@vrothberg it worked! Thank you for your help!

I had already noticed that [a section of the README](https://github.com/containers/skopeo#building-without-a-container) specifies the need for having the source directory in `$GOPATH/src/github.com/containers/skopeo` for *building*. But I wasn't expecting `vndr` to require it too, since I expected it to kind of only "grep" the source code.
